titleStmt.title: Letter from Lady Henrietta MacDonnell to Lady Clonbrock, 23 September 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Lady Henrietta MacDonnell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Lady MacDonnell of the Irish Women's Association to Lady Clonbrock (1839-1928) on the aid to Mesopotamia that was received by the Connaught Rangers. MacDonnell writes that she also regrets that they didn't have time to catch up while Lady Clonbrock.Augusta Caroline Dillon (née Crofton) was the wife of Luke Gerald Dillon (1834-1917), the 4th Baron of Clonbrock, Co. Galway and the daughter of Lord Crofton of Mote Park (Edward Henry Churchill Crofton, 3rd Baron), Co. Roscommon. Aged 75 at the outbreak of war, Lady Clonbrock, worked closely with the Irish Women's Association to send basic necessities to Irish POWs. Many of her care packages went to members of the Connaught Rangers imprisoned in Limburg near Cologne.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Pre-printed
Patronesses.
THE MARCHIONESS OF SLIGO
THE MARCHIONESS OF ORMONDE
THE DOWAGER MARCHIONESS OF
DUFFERIN AND AVA
THE COUNTESS OF SHAFTESBURY

THE COUNTESS OF ANTRIM

THE COUNTESS OF BANDON
THE COUNTESS OF KENMARE
THE COUNTESS OF LIMERICK
THE COUNTESS OF KERRY
THE VISCOUNTESS GORMANSTON
THE VISCOUNTESS POWERSCOURT
THE VISCOUNTESS BRYCE
THE LADY ARDEE
THE VISCOUNTESS ST.CYRES
THE LADY GWENDOLEN GUINNESS
THE LADY CECILIE GOFF
THE LADY CLONBROCK
THE LADY TEMPLEMORE
THE LADY ARDILAUN
THE LADY RUSSELL OF KILLOWEN
THE LADY BARRYMORE
LADY WELDON
LADY FITZGERLAD (OF VALENCIA)
LADY FITZGERLAD-ARNOTT
LADY PÃE
LADY BUTLER
LADY (EDWARD) HENRY
LADY MATHEW
MADAM MACDERMOT
MRS. JOHN REDMOND
MRS. COOPER
MRS. DEASE
MRS. LOFTUS
MISS EMILY HICKEY
MISS ELEANOR HULL
MISS MAHAFFY
President.
THE LADY MACDONNELL OF SWINFORD
3, BUCKINGHAM GATE,
LONDON, S.W.
Vice-President.
MRS. BRYANT, D.Sc., LITT.D.
6, ELDON ROAD, HAMPSTEAD,
LONDON, N.W.
Executive Committee.
MRS. JOHN BOLAND
MRS. CRILLY
MISS FITZGERALD
MISS MARGARET GOUGH
LADY (EDWARD) HENRY
MISS K. MCDONNELL
MISS RETA OLDHAM
MRS. LOUGH, Hon. Treas.
HON. ANNE MACDONNELL, Hon. Sec
Hon. Auditors.
MESSRS. WRIGHT, WAKE, PRICE & CO.
Chartered Accountants. The Irish Women's Association
(to aid Irish Regiments and Prisoners of War) Kensington Palace, London, W. Telephone No. Park 4139. September 23. 1916. Dear Lady Clonbrock, We have just received a
letter, of which I enclose you a copy, from
the Major of the Connaught Rangers in Mesopoâ
tamia. They seem very pleased indeed with
their parcels, and I see from the our enclosed
forms, which they return, that they all
arrived on the 12th. July. I hope you are keeping well and Lord
Clonbrock also. It was a great disappointment
to me to see so little of you when you were
in town, but the Association now takes all my
time, and I never see my friends, even those
who are living in London. Yours very sincerely, Henrietta MacDonnell
